title: Palindromic Liouvillian symmetry under dephasing: proof, spectral decomposition, and quantum state transfer
description: We prove that for N-qubit systems with Heisenberg (XXZ) coupling on any graph topology, subject to local Z-dephasing, the Liouvillian spectrum has an exact palindromic symmetry. The conjugation operator Pi acts per site (I to X, X to I, Y to iZ, Z to iY) and satisfies Pi L Pi^{-1} = -L - 2 sum(gamma_i) I. Every decay rate d is paired with a partner at 2 sum(gamma) - d. Verified numerically through N=7 (16384x16384 Liouvillian) across star, chain, ring, complete graph, and binary tree topologies. Individual observable components decay at specific palindromic rates: purity near 2 gamma, coherence near 10 gamma/3, concurrence envelope near 8 gamma/3. Applied to quantum state transfer: a star topology with 2:1 receiver-to-sender coupling ratio achieves F_avg = 0.888, outperforming standard chain configurations. The palindrome theorem applies to every Heisenberg spin chain with Z-dephasing studied in the QST literature.
